Every third loop in the next maturity phase must also run a RES-001 research-to-task gap review and apply RES-002 when maturing SaaS/OS operating surfaces; combine research and launch reviews when they coincide. If architecture/proof/evidence-heavy loops repeat and the owner flags missing scenario/interface progress, the next no-proof fallback must improve a scenario journey, runtime interaction, conditional L3 viewframe, or real-data/BFF slice before another architecture-only artifact.

post30Convergence.principle: Reach the final target in the fewest additional loops.
selectionRules: Choose only the shortest-path launch blocker.
Prefer end-to-end actor journey completion over new surface expansion.
Avoid exploratory, cosmetic, broad-refactor, or documentation-only work unless it directly removes a launch blocker.
Stop only for high-risk approval rules, product decisions, or blocking external state.

appliesWhen: auth, permission, security, public output, deployment, persistence, or external integration work
new UI pattern, member settings, admin workflow, or frontstage journey
framework, provider, browser, API, or library behavior may have changed
reference websites or comparable products can improve implementation quality
SaaS/OS operating surface maturity, real-data progression, records/audit, or agent API/CLI alignment is being designed
requiredOutput: official docs or reference implementations reviewed when relevant
executable task scope, acceptance criteria, likely files, verification, and risks
source links or local file references in the evidence report
score and level recorded before implementation
same page issue held constant across required rounds
distinct lens recorded for each round
selected and rejected implementation patterns recorded
executable task shape created only after required rounds
